request_text: Customers can pay with their debit card
rationale: Clause 2.2 includes accepting payments by credit card and debit card.

request_text: Let a shopper see how far along their purchase is
rationale: Paraphrase of order status tracking, included by Clause 2.1.

request_text: Group similar items so people can browse by type
rationale: Paraphrase of category filtering in the product catalog, included by Clause 2.3.

request_text: Let customers pay with the USDT stablecoin
rationale: Cryptocurrency payment is excluded by Clause 6.1.

request_text: Let outside vendors sell their products through our shop
rationale: Paraphrase of third-party marketplace seller integration, excluded by Clause 6.1.

request_text: Add seller ratings to product pages for items sourced from partner merchants
rationale: Reads like a catalog feature (Clause 2.3) but presupposes marketplace sellers, which Clause 6.1 excludes.

request_text: Send an email confirmation when an order ships
rationale: Near the orders clause (2.1) but transactional email notifications are a capability the contract never grants.

request_text: Let shoppers save their card for one-click reordering
rationale: Near payments (2.2) and cart (2.1) but stored payment credentials and one-click ordering are new capabilities.
Clause 2.2 supports integration with at most 3 payment providers; five exceeds the contractual limit.

request_text: Add another payment gateway to the checkout
rationale: Payment provider integration falls under Clause 2.2, whose 30-hour effort pool is exhausted by the logged work items.

request_text: Product search returns no results for words typed in uppercase
rationale: Defect report against delivered functionality; bug-fix maintenance is in scope per Clause 7.1. Deliberately shares vocabulary with Clause 2.3.
Too vague to map to any clause; the copilot should escalate to the PMO rather than guess.
